reviews.review_title: The vampires sucked the life out of this
reviews.review_description: 22 Feb '26 update: This is still too early for early access. Guns feel like water pistols, there is zero impact. Enemies are bullet sponges and have no reaction to being shot which is very unsatisfying. SFX is almost nonexistent, you can't even hear the enemies approaching from any direction. Abilities are very weak. Level design is bland. I keep getting stuck in objects/walls which makes bosses frustrating as you need to dodge a lot. The only positive is the weapon design, weapon switching, equipping menu and the skill trees. 5 months later it's still 2 stars at most, the gunplay just isn't fun which is the core of the game.

The game is not friendly for left handed players.  In the settings it asks what your dominant hand it but it has no bearing on tje actual gameplay.  Can't change the movement stick, spawn weapons in my left hand or change the virus powers to my right hand.
Also the reload mechanic is disappointing.

developer_response.body: Good afternoon, thanks for your feedback, and right now, for left-handed players, we only have pointer switching. Theoretically, we can also swap the shift sic and the turn stick. Unfortunately, the game wasn't made to switch all the functions to the left hand, plus narratively we also can't swap the hand that we use the skills and the hand that spark is on. If you want to discuss this issue in more detail, we will be glad to see you in our Dixotomia VR discord channel.

reviews.review_description:
A fast-paced shooter with cool visuals and buzz soundtracks. It's quite a difficult game for a beginner, I had to upgrade a couple of times to complete the mission..
A worthy project, played in one go!
Special praise for the visual of the weapon, which changes with increasing shooting range, I really liked it!
Keep it up, I look forward to further development of the project

developer_response.body: Thank you for the awesome review! We’re thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the fast-paced action, visuals, and soundtrack, and that the game kept you hooked enough to play it in one go. We know the difficulty can be challenging at first, especially for newcomers, so it’s great to hear that upgrading and progression helped you push through. Special thanks for pointing out the weapon visuals and how they evolve with range — that’s a detail we’re really proud of, and it means a lot that you noticed and liked it. Thanks again for your support and encouragement. We’ll keep pushing forward and can’t wait to show you what’s coming next!

reviews.review_description:
Tibet Factory pink screen seems to have been fixed.

However in terms of immersion it feels weird to drop guns on the ground each time i need my hands free. Would be good to be able to place it somewhere. Over shoulder or something.

Also another bug with weapon ammo. Even when upgrading weapon - it said total ammo count should increase. But for assault rifle for example max immortal stuck at 240 after upgrades. Also when shield gets upgraded - it is stuck at previous value until damage taken. Only then it will go to full strength.

Another issue is that weapon manipulation feels sluggish. And while it can make sense when one handing, when 2 handing rifle or shotgun - manipulation should be snappy.

developer_response.body: When updating weapons, the permissible ammunition increases, then you need to think about whether you need to immediately increase the ammunition (the number of rounds in the ammunition) when pumping. Regarding switching weapons, I think we can improve this moment somehow, if you have any suggestions, let's discuss them in the DixotomiaVR discord. regarding the weight of the weapon, if I understood correctly, then with a two-handed grip it should move faster than with a one-handed grip, this is valid, let's try to introduce additional coefficients for this! Thank you for giving feedback!

reviews.review_description:
Merci Lionel!
Even for Quest stand alone, this shows amazing graphics. I can feel the developer's hard work. I like that the story goes on rather than just continuous shooting. Following the story, there are various stages and diverse sorts of finely designed huge weapons, so I'm having fun playing.
There's a small wish that the cut scenes would be played in 3D. I was often caught during a physical collision with surrounding obstacles and stuck by it. It would be nice to have a little repulsive reaction in case of a physical collision. As other reviewers have written, if there's a little reaction when the enemy is shot and a little fragmented reaction when I'm hit, I think there'll be more immersion.

developer_response.body:
That’s fantastic — thank you so much for your thoughtful and detailed review!
Feel free to join our Discord channel Dixotomia VR so we can discuss your ideas further. Let’s make the game even better together!

reviews.review_title: Stylish AA Sci-Fi with Strong Atmosphere
reviews.review_description:
Dixotomia doesn’t try to pretend it’s a huge AAA production — and honestly, that works in its favor. Some levels make it clear right away that this is more of a double-A VR title, with compact spaces and a tighter scope. But what it may lack in scale, it more than makes up for with a strong sci-fi identity and satisfying moment-to-moment gameplay.

On Quest 3, the game runs well and delivers punchy combat, mixing firearms with supernatural abilities in a way that feels fun and deliberate. The world has a gritty, oppressive tone that really pulled me in, giving off Doom 3-style tension, flashes of Prototype’s biotech chaos, and a bit of that dystopian edge you’d expect from Cyberpunk 2077. It’s not massive or overly polished, but the vibe is absolutely there.

The characters and story help sell the setting too — especially Vivienne, who is comically similar to Mom from Futurama. The resemblance is so strong it’s hard not to smile every time she shows up.

Overall, Dixotomia feels like a confident AA VR shooter with a clear vision. If you’re on Quest 2 or 3 and appreciate strong atmosphere, sci-fi worldbuilding, and solid combat over sheer scale, it’s a worthwhile experience.

developer_response.body: Thank you for such a thoughtful and well-articulated review! We really appreciate how clearly you captured what we’re aiming for with Dixotomia. You’re absolutely right that we’re not trying to be a massive AAA production, and it means a lot to hear that the focused scope and moment-to-moment gameplay worked in the game’s favor for you. We’re glad to hear that performance on Quest 3 felt solid and that the blend of firearms and supernatural abilities came across as deliberate and satisfying. Your impressions of the atmosphere and tone are especially encouraging, creating a gritty, oppressive sci-fi vibe is a core pillar of the experience, so it’s great to know that it resonated. And yes, the comparison you made regarding Vivienne definitely gave us a smile. We’re happy the characters and story helped sell the setting and left a memorable impression. Thank you for recommending the game and for appreciating its vision. Your feedback and support mean a lot to us as we continue to refine and expand the experience.

reviews.review_description:
The game is amazing!
Great music and visuals. Awesome guns (especially the two-handed ones). Enemies intelligently take cover. The setting is unique and cool. The game is moderately challenging, which is good.
I had a problem with camera collision once, but it was a minor issue.
When you want to equip another weapon from your inventory, twist your hand. I didn't understand this until I reached a difficult scene.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the great review! We’re really happy to hear that you enjoyed the music, visuals, and weapons, especially the two-handed guns. It’s great to know the enemy behavior and overall challenge level felt just right for you. Thanks as well for mentioning the minor camera collision issue and the note about equipping weapons. Feedback like this is very helpful for us as we continue polishing the experience and improving clarity around mechanics. We really appreciate your support and are glad you’re enjoying the game!

reviews.review_title: THIS SHOULD BE TAKEN OUT OF THE STORE!
reviews.review_description:
I tried the game on an Oculus 3s.
After I started the game, it crashed my Oculus during the intro.  After a reinstall the menus to start the game disappeared.  Finally,  after a 3rd install I was able to play the game. 5 minutes later, after going through a door I was put in limbo, a place
 paat the boundaries of the play space,  that wasn't part of the game,  where I was stuck and couldn't make any progress.  After at least 10 game restarts and 30 minutes,  I was able to continue with the game.  10 minutes later,  I get stuck again because I get to a place where I am told to use an "ability " that is not in my inventory but should be.  The game wouldn't even let me physically move until I use said "ability" that I didn't have and couldn't  get. So, after 15 resets the ability finally appears, but I still can't get past the stage because another issue stops me.  THIS GAME IS LITERALLY UNPLAYABLE AND SHOULD BE TAKEN OUT OF THE PLAY STORE!

Apparently the developers are scamming their customers.  The good reviews for this game have to be totally bogus,  or from another platform form.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to leave a review and share your experience. We’re sorry to hear that you ran into issues while playing.

We’ve investigated the problem you mentioned and have already released a new patch that includes both new improvements and an additional fix specifically addressing this area. We hope this update allows you to continue progressing and enjoy the game as intended.

If you encounter any other issues or have feedback, we’d be happy to help. You’re also very welcome to join our Discord community, where we can respond more quickly to emerging problems and gather feedback directly from players.

Thank you again for your support, and we hope you’ll give the updated version a try

reviews.review_description: First of all, I was surprised about game's size. With just 4G, it has labs, observatory, military facility, and mines!!! Such various kinds of enemies, every combats are quite intense and versatile. Boss fights are interesting, because there are exosuite, vampires, and vampiric dragon. And also there are some special weapons across maps, it makes game easy to proceed. Music is very suitable to scenes and battles. I hope to play DLC or second Dixotomia installment in some point of future.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the great review! We’re really glad you were pleasantly surprised by the amount of content and variety packed into the game. Hearing that the different locations, enemy types, and intense combat stood out to you means a lot to us. It’s awesome to know you enjoyed the boss fights and the mix of enemies, as well as discovering special weapons throughout the maps. We’re also happy the music felt fitting for the scenes and battles, atmosphere is a big focus for us. Thank you for your support and enthusiasm for the future of the project. We’d love to bring more content to the world of Dixotomia, and we hope you’ll be there to experience it when the time comes.

reviews.review_description: i installed the game and as soon as i did and got to the part where i can start walking, i realized that i was going backwards into the left. thinking that i was having a problem with my left joy stick, i want to my desktop and found that the controller was moving normally. so i decided to  uninstall the game so i deleted the game and reinstalled the it. to my amazement it did NOT fix it! i then went into another game and saw that i was having no movement problems. my only option now is that the game itself is bad. i have ruled my controller has no issues and is working normally.  it would sure be nice if the developers could take a look into this issue please and see if they can find a fix for this issue. thank you

developer_response.body: Hello, this is completely unacceptable, let's figure out what's wrong with you and we'll fix it right away, in real time. But for a better understanding, could you write or upload a video on our DixotomiaVR discord channel about how the problem manifests itself. We carefully test versions and fix bugs quickly, but we haven't encountered such a problem yet. Write to any channel or personally to the administrator and we will solve this problem!

reviews.review_title: A Bold Mix of Vampires and Dark Sci-Fi
reviews.review_description:
Dixotomia was a very pleasant surprise, both in terms of atmosphere and gameplay. The game boldly blends vampire aesthetics, cyberpunk, and a touch of dark, cosmic sci-fi, creating a distinctive and memorable world of its own.

It’s one of those rare VR shooters that feels dynamic and engaging while avoiding overly complex or exhausting mechanics. Controls are intuitive, weapons feel different from each other, and there’s enough variety to experiment and have fun. Shooting feels satisfying and well paced.

Of course, there are still things that can be polished further – you can feel that the project has room to grow. That said, even in its current state the game is already very solid, with a clear vision, a strong foundation, and great potential for future development. It’s the kind of game you want to come back to and follow as it evolves.

The atmosphere deserves special mention – the visual style and overall mood work extremely well for immersion and make the project feel cohesive and authored, rather than just a collection of mechanics.

If you enjoy vampires, cyberpunk, and dark sci-fi themes, I highly recommend giving it a try. It’s clear that a lot of passion and ideas went into this game, and it definitely deserves support.

developer_response.body: Thank you for such a thoughtful and in-depth review! We’re truly happy to hear that the game was a pleasant surprise for you and that the blend of vampires, cyberpunk, and dark sci-fi resonated as something distinctive and memorable. Creating a cohesive, authored world with a strong atmosphere is one of our core goals, so your words mean a lot to us. We’re glad you enjoyed the dynamic gameplay, intuitive controls, and weapon variety, and that the shooting felt satisfying without becoming overly complex or exhausting. Your note about the game having room to grow is absolutely fair, and we see that potential the same way you do. We’re actively working on polishing and expanding the experience while staying true to the original vision. Special thanks for highlighting the atmosphere and immersion. Hearing that the project feels cohesive and driven by a clear creative vision is incredibly motivating for the team. Thank you for the recommendation and for supporting the game. We’re excited to keep developing it and hope you’ll continue to follow its evolution.

reviews.review_description: Now I looked at the game pictures and the trailer for the game and thought, “okay this looks pretty good” then I looked at the reviews saying that it was pretty good and so I went and bought it. Then when I loaded in I was met with disappointment, I don’t know if I was on lower grade hardware or if the people in the reviews are lying but the games graphics are terrible compared to the trailer, maybe something was wrong with my version but idk, the game was pretty misleading and not that fun.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we are sorry that you got a negative experience, we understand that the feeling of the game is the main thing that we have to give, unfortunately they did not match your expectations. Nevertheless, the opinion of each player is important to us in order to get better. We will be glad if you join our discord and tell us in more detail what you didn't like. Sincerely, the Dixotomia development team

reviews.review_description: The game is interesting, and the gunplay feels solid. There are some bugs here and there, but that’s normal for a pre-release version — nothing game-breaking so far. The visuals really pull you into the action, and you can sense the work of a skilled team

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review! We’re happy to hear that you found the gunplay solid and the overall experience engaging.
We appreciate your understanding regarding the bugs — we’re actively working on fixing and polishing everything as development continues. It’s great to know that the visuals helped pull you into the action and that the team’s work came through. Thanks for your support!

reviews.review_title: Here is the deal it’s buggy but?….
reviews.review_description: I bought this game in its early stages and became extremely vexed when I saw the flaws, however after speaking with the devs (only 2 by the way) they took my criticisms onboard and worked hard to do better, not many small teams who will do that these days, but if you want a sci-fi experience with decent gunplay and a few unique rpg elements and don’t care about certain AI content used?, THIS IS WORTH THE PRICE….. it’s not perfect but what the hell is ??!!.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing this, it really means a lot to us. We truly appreciate you sticking with the project, being honest about the flaws early on, and giving us the chance to improve. Taking player feedback seriously is very important to our small team, so it’s great to hear that our efforts to listen and iterate were noticeable. We’re glad you’re enjoying the sci-fi setting, gunplay, and RPG elements, even knowing the game isn’t perfect yet. Your understanding and support motivate us to keep refining and polishing the experience. Thanks again for believing in the project and for being part of its growth.

reviews.review_title: A Promising and Immersive VR Shooter
reviews.review_description: After spending some time with the game, it’s clear it’s heading in the right direction. The gunplay feels powerful and satisfying, with stylish futuristic weapons and engaging reload mechanics that work especially well in VR. The visuals are atmospheric and detailed, with strong worldbuilding and smart, aggressive enemies that keep the action tense and dynamic. While it’s still in early access and has a few rough edges, nothing breaks the experience. The developers’ progress is noticeable, and the game already stands as a solid, immersive VR shooter well worth your time.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the thoughtful review! We’re really glad to hear that you feel the game is moving in the right direction and that the gunplay, weapons, and reload mechanics stood out as satisfying in VR. It’s great to know that the atmosphere, worldbuilding, and enemy behavior helped create a tense and dynamic experience. We appreciate your understanding regarding the remaining rough edges of Early Access, and it means a lot that the progress is noticeable. Thanks for your support and recommendation. We’ll keep working to make the game even better as development continues.

reviews.review_description:
I played Dixotomia for a while. It does have bugs and rough edges  but I liked the aesthetic. It evokes memories of 1994's Maraphon and Doom 3, has a potential to become a great action game with a dense atmosphere once finished. I'd love to see more narrative packed into it with next updates. And bugfixes, yeah.

Eager to see this game in it's final form

developer_response.body: Thank you for the thoughtful review! We’re glad the aesthetic resonated with you, and it’s especially nice to hear the comparisons to Marathon and Doom 3 — those are great references and very much in the spirit of what we’re aiming for. We appreciate your honest notes about the rough edges and bugs, and we’re actively working on polishing the experience. Adding more narrative depth is also something we plan to expand on in future updates. Thanks for your interest and support. We’re excited to keep improving the game and hope you’ll enjoy seeing it grow into its final form.

reviews.review_description: I gave this game a two star because I didn’t wanna be too harsh. But the game has a few flaws, especially if you end up going back into another room that you previously visited sometimes it won’t let you go forward. Weapon mechanics need to be revisited as well because the scopes aren’t that accurate. Also weapon selection is quite complicated. You can’t switch between the handgun and the rifle really easily from the beginning. I think I bought this game beginning of January 2026 and I tried to play it yesterday and it still has all the same problems. Plus, I don’t think it can crouch at all so if you drop something, it is hard to pick it up unless you’ve bend over and even then sometimes it won’t let you pick it up ￼￼￼

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, you clearly reflected your emotions. It is important for us to understand the players' feelings. Yes, some mechanics don't seem convenient or unusual for viar games, but we tried to make them as comfortable and convenient as possible! In any case, if you have the desire and the opportunity, I invite you to our discord channel DixotomiaVR so that we can discuss the problems and potentially make the game better! Thanks!

reviews.review_description: this game a really bad, nothing like advertised, its weak with clean visual graphic.

developer_response.body:
Hey Jahryder, thanks for trying Dixotomia and for leaving feedback. Sorry to hear the gameplay didn’t match your expectations.
Dixotomia is a fully finished and polished game, but we still care a lot about player feedback and are always looking for ways to improve the experience. Since you gave Dixotomia a 1-star review, we’d really like to understand what exactly disappointed you so much in the game and how long you played it.
You also mentioned that the game is “weak with clean visual graphic.” Could you clarify what you meant by that? What specifically felt wrong about the graphics or visual style?
If you’re open to it, please join our Discord and share your thoughts with us directly. We’d be happy to discuss it and better understand your perspective.

Discord: DixotomiaVR

Thanks again for giving the game a try.

reviews.review_description:
The setting is really unique — this blend of high‑tech and technomagic creates such a cool atmosphere, and the story’s actually fun to follow.
The gameplay’s super varied: you can go all‑out with the shooting, then chill out with some dialogue and world exploration. The graphics are stylish, nothing to complain about there. As you play, you keep getting new stuff — skills, weapons, enemies — so it never feels stale.
Balance is a bit all over the place sometimes: certain abilities feel way too strong, but then other fights get surprisingly tough because enemies switch up their tactics. I’ve been following this game for a while now, and I can see the devs are listening to feedback — it’s getting better with every update!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the detailed and thoughtful review! We’re really glad you enjoyed the unique setting and the mix of high-tech sci-fi with technomagic — creating a distinctive atmosphere and a story worth following is very important to us. It’s great to hear that the variety in gameplay, from intense combat to dialogue and exploration, kept things fresh for you, and that the steady flow of new skills, weapons, and enemies helped maintain momentum. We also appreciate your honest notes on balance. Ability tuning and combat pacing are things we’re actively refining, especially as enemy behavior evolves. It means a lot that you’ve been following the project and noticed how player feedback is shaping the updates. Thanks for your support — we’ll keep working to make each update even better.

reviews.review_description: The initial launch was not the most polished experience let's say. But I've been watching major patches going live one after another, and finally decided to give it a second chance. Now it feels like the proper experience the devs promised way back. It's definitely a decent action shooter in visually rich surroundings. VR still lacks such games. Keep up the good work! Love the insides of characters' models btw :D

developer_response.body: Thank you for the thoughtful review and for giving the game a second chance. We really appreciate you sticking with us and noticing the progress made through the updates. It means a lot to hear that the experience now feels closer to what we originally promised. We are glad you are enjoying the action-focused gameplay and the visually rich environments, as VR definitely needs more games like this. We will keep working hard to make the game even better, and we are happy you noticed the attention to detail as well.

reviews.review_description:
A great game for young commanders. This is a single-player shooter with beautiful environments and a wide variety of weapons. The enemies are not just mindless targets — they are accurate, inventive, and keep you constantly on edge.

There is a solid tutorial at the start that helps you quickly understand the game’s mechanics. The story makes you care about the main character and adds real emotional involvement.

Probably the best proof of immersion is that my VR headset battery died while I was playing 🙂 I just couldn’t stop.
Highly recommended to buy!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the amazing review! We’re thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the fast-paced gameplay, beautiful environments, and variety of weapons. It’s great to know that the enemies felt challenging and kept you engaged throughout the experience.
We’re also glad the tutorial helped you quickly get into the mechanics and that the story created an emotional connection with the main character. Hearing that you were so immersed your VR headset ran out of battery is the best compliment we could ask for.
Thank you for the recommendation and for your support—we hope you continue to enjoy the game!

reviews.review_description: The game looks interesting, with a lot of well-thought-out details and good graphics, but you need to get used to the shooting, especially the grenade throwing. It's an exciting shooter that keeps you on the edge of your seat.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We’re glad you enjoyed the visuals and noticed the attention to detail in the environments. The combat, especially mechanics like grenade throwing, can take some time to get used to, so we appreciate you sticking with it. It’s great to hear that the intensity kept you engaged and on edge. Thanks for sharing your impressions and for playing!

reviews.review_title: Good but some odd design issues
reviews.review_description:
First off let me say that this is a solid standalone fps campaign. Definitely worth the $20. But it has some issues that i hope they fix.

Let me preface these issues with the fact that I am playing on a quest 2. The game looks fine and runs fine with minor dips. 

Issues:
- inability to crouch, with ammo crates on the floor that u cant reach without toggling seat/stand in the options menu
- distracting hud overlay. It reminds me of borderlands vr. It moves with ur headset and is distracting, breaking immersion.
- no way to holsters weapons, u just drop them. Its a minor complaint but weird.

I know it sounds like im complaining but I overall like the game and for $20 it has high production look and pretty fun gunplay. 

If they fix half of the above issues, id edit my review to 5 stars.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. You're right, having highlighted these problems, and I think we can fix half of them quickly enough) unfortunately, the holster mechanism requires key improvements, but we'll think about what we can do about it. Join our discord channel, where I will definitely write a patch as soon as we fix one of these problems.

reviews.review_description: I don’t know if there’s a bug in this game that won’t let me move in the quest three it’s just stuck on one place saying press X on your left controller, rotate your wrist and release your ability. I tried to do that every time it won’t do anything at all. It’s just stuck there.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review and for reporting the issue. We’re sorry for the inconvenience.

We’ve released a patch that includes new improvements and an additional fix for this specific problem. We hope this helps you continue and enjoy the game.

You’re also welcome to join our Discord, where we can respond faster to any issues or questions. Thanks again for your feedback!

reviews.review_title: A good bang for ur buck
reviews.review_description: I first played the game when it was in beta, back then it was pretty rough around the edges but know it’s really improved it’s self after the updates. Graphics are better and so is the combat system.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review! We’re really happy to hear that you’ve noticed how much the game has improved since the beta!
The team has put a lot of effort into enhancing the graphics and combat system, and feedback like this truly motivates us to keep making the game better. Thanks for sticking with us and supporting the game!

reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed the shooting, the beautiful levels, and the world's lore. It's still in early access, so there are some rough edges, but I'm really looking forward to the release. I highly recommend it!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review! We’re glad to hear you enjoyed the shooting mechanics, the visuals, and the world’s lore.
You’re absolutely right that Early Access still has some rough edges, and we’re actively working on polishing and improving the game. It means a lot to us that you’re looking forward to the full release, and thank you for the recommendation and support!

reviews.review_title: 2 stars for now, and that's lucky
reviews.review_description:
Most reviews are right about this, the other couple praising it up the state its in right now still, need there heads looking at use really do

It could be a great game, it has potential, i was having a bit and i mean a bit of a blast with it at first, but its just full of issues in every way possible, and the full release is meant to be round the corner in October, I wish them luck with that, as cannot see that happening as what the game should be at full release, I just cannot see that happening for October, I hope so, but can't see it

The devs do reply usually promptly on discord and have fixed a couple of issues and progression blockers pretty quick I have posted but also its caused more issues at the same time, and now theres another progression blocker so no point trying to play, and theres just been a recent update posted on discord yesterday so thought I'd try playing again but theres no update there 🤷‍♂️ , and so trying to play its still in the exact same state which I'm disappointed with tbh, hopefully the update will kick in tomorrow although if they announced it then it should be there but its not

And this maybe harsh to say, but a company like sanzaru should have made this game, they would have knocked it out the park from the get go, but I do hope these guys get this game fully fixed and sorted and polished soon as it could be a great game, so I'll keep my fingers crossed 🤞for these guys as with all the bad reviews hardly anyone will buy it

Once its fully finished and polished, then I'll come back to change my review on it and hopefully then it'll be worth more stars 🤞and a much better review

developer_response.body: Everything that is written here is true, and indeed, sometimes closing some bugs, others appear, nevertheless, we honestly and openly talk about it and do everything possible to prevent this from happening. Now each patch is not a couple of minor edits, it's a lot of work by the whole team, which includes bug fixes and the introduction of new mechanics and edits of the old ones to make them more convenient, as well as visual improvements, narrative, sound and more. Of course, this is not an excuse and it is disgusting when players face such problems. But we are honestly and openly talking about this and are ready to resolve all controversial issues in the discord. Thank you for coming with us side by side for the release, we will not disappoint!

reviews.review_title: With good prospects for post release
reviews.review_description: I have been following the team for a long time, it is great that the project has been developing all this time. Although vampire setting lately can be seen more often, but not in a while, because it is interesting to see what the result of the team.  So far this release is felt early, and because I hope that the guys will continue to support the project and bring its polishing to "shine". In the future and for setting high appreciation, will definitely come back to see what happened after release, well and add feedback ;)

I knew there was going to be a problem when I couldn't even click on the main menu right at the start to choose my options. After much clicking and reaching my arm out to try and make the pointer register I finally made it past that hurdle and into the game. Which is where  the first piece of advice was to use the menu button on my wrist to bring up the main menu, and that completely failed to work too. After multiple tries it again finally popped up and I was able to continue. I tried again and recorded a short clip and I just couldn't get it to work again.

Next, movement felt a bit slow and floaty, and the click to run option seems to have little effect whilst also randomly deciding whether to actually work or stay activated. This became more of an issue later when fighting enemies. But first I had to pick up a gun and armour then head to the shooting range. That was when I had my first interaction with a touch panel. Which has no physical touch mechanics. You can hold your hand near it or pass your whole hand through the panel like it doesn't exist.

At the range I found that the guns have no weight, and shoot bullets in a weird arcade style slow motion. Not great but not necessarily a deal breaker at first. Then the second wave in the tutorial was activated which generated moving holograms and that's where I found out how irritating it was to watch your bullets glide past your target because they are moving too slowly.

Persevering I played through the next few corridors and experienced the full handicap of the sluggish movement and slowmo bullets, whilst being jumped by enemies who seem to just randomly spawn out of nowhere, and who exhibit pretty weak AI, including two who walked right in front of my gun to get past me into cover that didn't actually hide them.

All the while everything felt slow, underpowered, and stale. The environment is stagnant, there's no ambient audio, just some music cues when enemies show up. I made it as far as picking up a wrist mounted shock weapon I forget the name of. It told me to wait until it was charged up. It just crackled (visually rather than audibly) with electricity and I had no idea if I was supposed to wait any longer so I fired it. Then it just crackled away and gave no indication of charging again. Headed out into the corridor, blasted a few enemies and it stopped working, with no further feedback to assist. Although it seems it saps your armour and I'd used mine up, based on the HUD, I just had no way of knowing the status of the weapon when in use.

After that I got jumped from behind by a large amount of enemies who again spawned from nowhere inside a fairly big room while I was in the middle of picking up ammo, so my health took a kicking and thanks to the sluggish movement I was unable to manoeuvre out if danger in time. At that point I'd had enough.

At no point was this enjoyable. It feels very unfinished in terms of gameplay and interaction. Visually its decent but at the same time it feels like a rather bland and dead environment. Overall I'd describe this as an early Alpha arcade game feel with more attention to looks than gameplay. I expect that if the gunplay, AI, movement and audio were all to improved it could be much better but as it stands it isn't IMO close to release worthy.

And I'm sorry if that upsets anyone but we are too far down the VR pipeline to accept content reminiscent of the early days, especially when there are other titles able to make these key elements work. I genuinely hope it gets a lot better.

developer_response.body:
Good day! Thank you for joining the early access. Feedback from each player is important to us and we will delve into and analyze all the problems described. We are sorry that you had a negative experience interacting with our game. But by the release, most of the problems you mentioned will be fixed, namely:
the interface and all interactions with it will not cause problems
we will add a sprint indicator and work out the acceleration and movement mechanism itself
bullet speed and everything related to shooting will be balanced and brought to convenient use
AI and shelters will work better and more interestingly
we will reduce the indicator and visual spark to a complete and understandable picture so that it is clearly understandable how to use it

And there will also be many other improvements for the release. If you can share your experience in more detail, we will be glad to see you in our DixotomiaVR discord channel, where we can watch your records and make the game even better and more interesting. Thank you!

reviews.review_description: Now the devs done some work on this , much better play ability. Still a few glitches. The graphics are great   Removed my one star original review. As so much better

developer_response.body: We are very pleased that you have changed your mind, your previous assessment was honest and fair, and I think the current assessment is the same. And this is very important to us, it is a huge incentive for the release to make sure that we get all 5 stars, so before the release we will put twice as much effort into making the game perfect.

reviews.review_description: I played this back when it was a demo & I have to say, even just into this Early Access, it's come a long way. It feels smoother, better looking & improved from the demo, which I guess is to be expected but it's just a really enjoyable story & concept with some pretty unique gameplay features/mechanics. I feel there aren't too many single player story based games released on VR, most VR games seem to be multiplayer, which I get & do enjoy, but it's really nice to have a purely single player based story game like this & I look forward to what more comes of the game in future & on full release. Give me more vampires, more weapons, more levels & I'm happy! Haven't experienced any bugs that are game breaking or experience ruining, there's one or two little things every now & again, a floating object etc but that's to be expected in early access & updates seem to be made regularly so it's clear the developer is working on these things, which I always appreciate.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the detailed and thoughtful review! We’re really happy to hear that you’ve noticed how far the game has come since the demo and that the improvements in visuals, smoothness, and overall polish stand out to you!
It means a lot to us that you enjoy the story, concept, and unique gameplay mechanics—especially as a single-player, story-driven VR experience. We agree that there aren’t enough games like that in VR, and it’s great to know this resonates with you.
Your feedback and enthusiasm for more vampires, weapons, and levels is definitely noted! We also appreciate your understanding regarding minor early access quirks, and we’re glad you can see that we’re actively working on updates and fixes.
Thanks again for your support, and we’re excited to bring you even more as development continues!

reviews.review_description: Im going to be honest, the game is alright. Controls are simple and easy to use, same with the guns and ability. The only problem that i see that might hold the game back is the graphics characters, if they clean it up. It could possibly give the NPC's in the game more of a human to it (that's my opinion though). But the game seems good for the most part. There was in any glitches or bugs when i was playing through. The concert of it is pretty cool, and it was fun to use a shotgun on the enemies.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the honest feedback! We’re glad to hear you found the controls intuitive and enjoyed the weapons and abilities—especially the shotgun!
We appreciate your thoughts on the character graphics as well. Improving NPC visuals and making them feel more lifelike is valuable feedback, and it’s something we’ll definitely keep in mind as development continues.
It’s also great to know you didn’t run into any bugs or glitches during your playthrough. Thanks for giving the game a try and sharing your impressions—we’re excited to keep improving the experience as we move forward!

reviews.review_description: The game looks decent, plays smoothly, and Early Access has shown how the game can change and improve during development. A lot of reviews have contributed to the development of Dixotomia and it is clear that the game has become much better. I really liked the visual style, the plot component, the narrative elements and the soundtrack. Something is constantly happening in the game and does not let me get bored, pumping makes me just a terminator, since I went to the techno branch, I will have to try to pump the virus branch completely. I expect that all minor bugs will be fixed by the release and cool features will be added.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your detailed review! We’re thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the visual style, story, narrative elements, and soundtrack!
It’s great to know that you’ve noticed the improvements in Early Access and that feedback from players has helped shape the game. We’re glad you’re enjoying the progression and experimenting with different branches—it’s always fun to see how players build their characters!
We’re actively working on fixing minor bugs and adding exciting new features, and we hope you’ll enjoy the full release even more. Your support and feedback mean a lot to us!

reviews.review_description: Graphics aren't bad I'll leave it at that.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review and for sharing your thoughts. We appreciate all feedback from players, as it helps us improve.
Since the start of development, we’ve put a lot of work into improving the game and continue to actively refine it. We hope that by the time of the full release, the changes will be even more noticeable, and you’ll give the game another chance to see how it has evolved.
